title: Question : Idempotent Payment System
description: Build a small service in Go that simulates a payment system with an idempotent POST /pay endpoint.
expected_behaviors: When a valid payment request is sent for a new transaction ID, it should return a transaction with status 'pending'.
The system should simulate processing and eventually transition the transaction status to 'processed'.
If the exact same transaction ID is sent again, it should return the same transaction (Idempotency), showing the current status (pending or processed).
If the same transaction ID is sent but with a different user ID or amount, it should reject the request to prevent conflicts.
If the amount is <= 0 or the transaction ID is empty, it should return a bad request error.

title: Question 2: Worker Pool Task Processor
description: Implement a worker pool that processes 100 tasks (squaring numbers) using only 5 concurrent workers, returning results in order.
expected_behaviors: Given 100 tasks and 5 workers, all tasks should be processed concurrently with max 5 workers running at once.
Results should be returned in the original task order (task 1, task 2, ..., task 100).
The execution time should demonstrate concurrency benefits compared to sequential processing.
Each result should show which worker processed it for transparency.
